mnemonics.title: TREE HIERARCHY REMEMBER
mnemonics.content: ROOT FIRST: Remember that a tree has a ROOT at the top, branches DOWNWARD like a real tree. Think: 'R' for Root at top, 'E' for Edges connect nodes, 'E' for Every node (except root) has one parent, 'A' for Ancestors go up, 'S' for Siblings share parent.

mnemonics.title: TREE TERMS MEMORY
mnemonics.content: LEAF HAS NO CHILDREN: Leaf = Lacks Children And Terminal (has no children). Think: 'LEAF' = Last (no children), End, And Finished.

mnemonics.title: BINARY TREE DEGREE
mnemonics.content: BINARY MEANS TWO: Binary = Bi (two) + ary (related to). Each node can have AT MOST 2 children - left and right.

acronyms.expansion: Depth First Search - Traversing a tree by going as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking
acronyms.usage: Used for tree traversal and searching

acronyms.expansion: Breadth First Search - Traversing a tree level by level from root to leaves
acronyms.usage: Used for level-order traversal and finding shortest path in unweighted trees

visualTips.visualization: Picture an organizational chart or family tree. The CEO/grandparent at top (root), managers/parents below, and employees/children at bottom. Just like an org chart, information flows downward.
Think of a real tree - the trunk (root) supports everything above, and the leaves at the tips are endpoints. In data trees, root is entry point, leaves are endpoints.
Height goes DOWN from a node (how far to lowest leaf). Depth goes UP from node to root. Height measures the tree's total reach downward, depth measures distance upward from root.
Imagine an inverted tree (root at top, branches down). A parent is closer to the root, children are further down. Information/commands flow from parent to children.

analogies.analogy: A linear structure like an array is like a single-file line of people. A tree is like an organizational hierarchy where one manager oversees several subordinates - one manager can have many reports.
The root is like the CEO of a company - the topmost person who everyone reports to eventually. No one is above the CEO.
Leaf nodes are like employees with no subordinates - they're at the bottom of the org chart with no one reporting to them.
A subtree is like a department within a company - it has its own manager (sub-root) and all the employees (descendants) under that manager.
